title: Contact dermatitis

red_flags.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports fever, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening or spreading painful eczema, infection concerns, and clinician-documented return fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 7 and 13 — red flags and referral criteria

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, and patch testing without recommending tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology

medication.text: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.
medication.rationale: Supports clinician-documented trigger avoidance, skin care, emollient, and topical-treatment decisions without medicines or doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 11–12 — clinician-led avoidance, skin care, and treatment

follow_up.text: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.
follow_up.rationale: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, redness, burning, blistering, exposure, distribution, morphology, and clinician-reviewed history or photographs.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

source_limitations: Mucosal involvement, facial swelling, fixed duration presets, fixed one-to-two-week review, and broad irritant-dermatitis equivalence remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer allergy, infection, trigger, medicine need, or referral urgency.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
